title: Measuring What Matters - TCR 04/18/26
description:
Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei met with White House chief of staff Susie Wiles in a meeting both sides called "productive and constructive," developer analytics firms published data showing AI-assisted code generates up to 9.4x higher churn rates than human-written code, and the largest onshore wind project in the United States began generating electricity.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-18-2026/

The administration that blacklisted Anthropic sixty days ago just called a meeting with its CEO "productive" - the most significant shift in the Pentagon confrontation since Judge Lin's March injunction. Anthropic launched Claude Design for slide decks, prototypes, and design systems while simultaneously negotiating government access to Mythos. Three senior OpenAI executives departed on the same day as the company released GPT-Rosalind, its first life sciences model, with Amgen, Moderna, and the Allen Institute as initial partners. Multiple analytics firms published converging evidence that AI-assisted code generates dramatically higher churn rates - up to 9.4x - than human-written code, with effective acceptance rates dropping from 80-90% on first pass to 10-30% after revision cycles. Cursor is nonetheless negotiating a $50 billion valuation on $2 billion ARR. Pattern Energy's SunZia wind farm began generating power from 916 turbines across 3.5 GW in New Mexico, connected to a 550-mile HVDC transmission line serving Arizona and California - the largest onshore wind project in the country at $8 billion.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-18-2026/
